title: Catalog Request Item Backlog and WIP
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" earliest=-14d
| where match(lower(state),"(?i)open|work in progress|approved|sc_work|waiting for approval")
| eval age_days=round((now()-sys_created_on)/86400,1)
| stats count as wip, median(age_days) as med_age_days by cat_item, assignment_group
| sort -wip
| head 50
description: A growing backlog of open request items signals fulfillment bottlenecks or catalog misconfiguration; monitoring WIP by catalog item supports request fulfilment SLAs and ITIL service request lifecycle discipline.
value: A growing backlog of open request items signals fulfillment bottlenecks or catalog misconfiguration; monitoring WIP by catalog item supports request fulfilment SLAs and ITIL service request lifecycle discipline.
grandmaExplanation: A growing backlog of open request items signals fulfillment bottlenecks or catalog misconfiguration ŌĆö so tickets, changes, and incidents are on track and nothing is silently missed.
implementation: (1) Normalize `state` values to your catalog workflow; (2) Exclude cancelled/closed states via `where`; (3) Review top `wip` lines weekly with fulfillment owners and feed automation candidates back to the service catalog team.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item"` (`state`, `cat_item`, `assignment_group`, `sys_created_on`).
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
(1) Normalize `state` values to your catalog workflow; (2) Exclude cancelled/closed states via `where`; (3) Review top `wip` lines weekly with fulfillment owners and feed automation candidates back to the service catalog team.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" earliest=-14d
| where match(lower(state),"(?i)open|work in progress|approved|sc_work|waiting for approval")
| eval age_days=round((now()-sys_created_on)/86400,1)
| stats count as wip, median(age_days) as med_age_days by cat_item, assignment_group
| sort -wip
| head 50
```

Understanding this SPL

**Catalog Request Item Backlog and WIP** ŌĆö A growing backlog of open request items signals fulfillment bottlenecks or catalog misconfiguration; monitoring WIP by catalog item supports request fulfilment SLAs and ITIL service request lifecycle discipline.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item"` (`state`, `cat_item`, `assignment_group`, `sys_created_on`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:sc_req_item. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ŌĆó Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(state),"(?i)open|work in progress|approved|sc_work|waiting for approval")` ŌĆö typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
ŌĆó `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** ŌĆö often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
ŌĆó `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cat_item, assignment_group** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
ŌĆó Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (WIP by catalog item), Table (top queues with median age), Heatmap (assignment_group ├Ś cat_item).
visualization: Bar chart (WIP by catalog item), Table (top queues with median age), Heatmap (assignment_group ├Ś cat_item).
